column6: Webb's operationalization refers to reactive avoidance rather than deliberate planning
Webb's operationalization refers to rumination (stimulus-driven) rather than deliberate attentional control
Structured actions to change situation features to manage emotion
Typically involves applying a procedure; limited integrative meaning-making
Reappraises a experience or some situations requires integration of emotion with context
Requires having multiple viewpoints to re-interpret the context of the emotional event; relational, meaning-focused processing
Cognitive changes typically involve reappraising meaning beyond following a rule
Changing someone’s attitudes or judgements requires a meta-cognitive shifting; rather than just suppressing it
